named_technique: Evertse-Schlickewei-Schmidt 2002 (Annals) S-unit bound on fixed-support pairs
bridge_lemma: Fix a finite set of primes S. Solutions to œÜ(n)=œÜ(n+1) with supp(n)‚à™supp(n+1)‚äÜS are encoded as S-unit equations u+v=1 in the multiplicative group ‚ü®S‚ü© ‚äÇ ‚Ñö*; by the Evertse-Schlickewei-Schmidt 2002 form of the Subspace Theorem, the number of non-degenerate solutions is bounded by a function only of |S| and rank(‚ü®S‚ü©), independent of the heights of n and n+1. Intersecting with the torus constraint œÜ(n)=œÜ(n+1) cannot increase that count, so the per-support set is finite.
informal_proof_outline: Single-pillar attack on the FINITENESS sub-claim of Erd≈ës 1003 (NOT the infinitude direction). This iter2 reformulation explicitly drops the speculative Tao-2016 entropy decrement pillar that Aristotle rejected in v1 (UUID 4697679f-3c66-4f46-a156-7f9900dfdf22) on the grounds that Tao's method addresses Chowla, not pairs (œÜ(n), œÜ(n+1)). Setup. Let S be a finite set of primes. Suppose n>0 satisfies œÜ(n)=œÜ(n+1) and supp(n)‚à™supp(n+1)‚äÜS. Write n = ‚àè_{p‚ààS} p^{a_p} and n+1 = ‚àè_{q‚ààS} q^{b_q}, where a_p,b_q ‚â• 0 (with a_p>0 only for p‚ààsupp(n), similarly for b_q). Reduction to an S-unit equation. The identity (n+1) ‚àí n = 1 becomes ‚àè q^{b_q} ‚àí ‚àè p^{a_p} = 1, i.e. setting u := ‚àè q^{b_q} and v := ‚àí‚àè p^{a_p} ‚àà ‚ü®S‚ü© ‚äÇ ‚Ñö*, we have u + v = 1 with u,v lying in the finitely generated multiplicative group Œì := ‚ü®S‚ü© ‚äÇ ‚Ñö* of rank r=|S|. Application of Evertse-Schlickewei-Schmidt. The Annals 2002 theorem asserts: the number of non-degenerate solutions (u,v) ‚àà Œì¬≤ to a_1 u + a_2 v = 1 (for fixed nonzero a_1,a_2 ‚àà ‚Ñö*) is bounded by an explicit function of the dimension 2 and the rank r of Œì. In our case a_1=a_2=1, so the count of pairs (u,v) is finite with bound depending only on |S|. Each pair determines (n, n+1) uniquely (n = ‚àív, n+1 = u), so the count of consecutive pairs is finite. The torus constraint. The equality œÜ(n)=œÜ(n+1) is an additional constraint, namely ‚àè_{p‚ààS}(p‚àí1)p^{a_p‚àí1}¬∑[a_p>0] = ‚àè_{q‚ààS}(q‚àí1)q^{b_q‚àí1}¬∑[b_q>0]. This is a polynomial identity in the integer exponents (a_p, b_q)_{p,q‚ààS}; combined with the S-unit equation, it cuts down (or at worst preserves) the already finite set. Hence the intersection is finite. Conclusion. For every finite set S of primes, the set {n > 0 : œÜ(n)=œÜ(n+1), supp(n(n+1))‚äÜS} is finite. This is a provable strengthening of Erd≈ës-Pomerance-S√°rk√∂zy 1987 to per-support effectiveness; it makes no claim about the infinitude direction, which remains the genuine open conjecture. Honest scope. This sub-claim is the realistic Aristotle target: it converts the Diophantine pillar of the prior approach into a stand-alone finiteness theorem, ungated by any entropy-decrement assumption. Aristotle verified 7 specific solutions (n=1,3,15,104,164,194,255) sorry-free in v1; the iter2 sub-claim is logically independent of, and strictly weaker than, the full infinitude statement.
candidate_lemmas: sunit_pair_from_phi: From œÜ(n)=œÜ(n+1) and supp(n(n+1))‚äÜS, derive the pair (u,v) = ((n+1)/N, ‚àín/N) ‚àà Œì_S¬≤ with u+v = 1/N for some integer N ‚àà ‚ü®S‚ü©; equivalently, multiply through so that u+v=1 in the rational S-unit group.
sunit_group_rank: For S a finite set of primes with |S|=r, the multiplicative group ‚ü®S‚ü© ‚äÇ ‚Ñö* has rank r, generated by the primes in S.

evertse_schlickewei_schmidt_2002: The number of non-degenerate solutions (x_1,x_2) ‚àà Œì¬≤ to a_1 x_1 + a_2 x_2 = 1, with Œì a finite-rank subgroup of (K*)^2 (K char-0 field) of rank r, is bounded by an explicit function c(2,r), independent of a_1,a_2 ‚àà K*. (Annals 2002, DOI 10.2307/3062133.)
non_degeneracy_for_consecutive: Solutions (u,v) corresponding to a consecutive pair (n,n+1) with n>0 are non-degenerate (no proper subsum is zero) since u,v ‚àà ‚Ñö* are both nonzero.

torus_constraint_polynomial: The œÜ-equality ‚àè_{p‚ààS}(p‚àí1)p^{a_p‚àí1}¬∑[a_p>0] = ‚àè_{q‚ààS}(q‚àí1)q^{b_q‚àí1}¬∑[b_q>0] is a finite polynomial constraint on the exponent vector (a_p,b_q)_{p,q‚ààS}, hence cuts down a finite set to a (possibly empty) finite subset.
pair_to_integer_recovery: Each non-degenerate S-unit pair (u,v) with u+v=1 in ‚ü®S‚ü© recovers at most one (n, n+1) via n+1 = u¬∑N, n = ‚àív¬∑N, where N is the common denominator in ‚ü®S‚ü©.
fixed_support_finite_main: For any finite set S of primes, the set {n > 0 : œÜ(n)=œÜ(n+1) ‚àß supp(n)‚à™supp(n+1)‚äÜS} is finite.
honest_disclaimer: iter2 of E1003 (prior_iter_uuid 4697679f-3c66-4f46-a156-7f9900dfdf22) explicitly drops the speculative entropy-decrement pillar Aristotle correctly critiqued in v1: 'Tao 2016 addresses Chowla, not this; needs an entropy gain stronger than published'. This sketch targets only the FINITENESS direction per fixed support, which IS provable from the Subspace Theorem (Evertse-Schlickewei-Schmidt 2002 Annals, DOI 10.2307/3062133; MR 1923966; Zbl 1026.11038). It does NOT directly imply the infinitude conjecture of Erd≈ës 1003 ‚Äî that direction remains open and would require an unbounded-support mechanism. The known sporadic solutions (n=1,3,15,104,164,194,255,...) all have small supports and are captured by this finiteness theorem as a non-vacuous statement. Mathlib gap: the Evertse-Schlickewei-Schmidt quantitative form is not in Mathlib; Aristotle may need to either invoke it as a citation-level black box or scaffold the qualitative (Schmidt 1971-style) Subspace Theorem version sufficient for finiteness alone.
